{@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\\n183:     public WindowOperator(\\n184:             WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\\n185:             TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\\n186:             KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\\n187:             TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\\n188:             StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\\n189:             InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\\n190:             Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\\n191:             long allowedLateness,\\n192:             OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\\n193: \\n194:         super(windowFunction);\\n195: \\n196:         checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\\n197: \\n198:         checkArgument(\\n199:                 windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\\n200:                 \\\"window state serializer is not properly initialized\\\");\\n201: \\n202:         this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\\n203:         this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\\n204:         this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\\n205:         this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\\n206:         this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\\n207:         this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\\n208:         this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\\n209:         this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\\n210:     }\\n211: \\n212:     @Override\\n213:     public void setup(\\n214:             StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\\n215:             StreamConfig config,\\n216:             Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\\n217:         super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\\n218:     }\\n219: \\n220:     @Override\\n221:     public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\\n222:         super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\\n223:     }\\n224: \\n225:     @Override\\n226:     public void open() throws Exception {\\n227:         super.open();\\n228: \\n229:         this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\\n230:         timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\\n231: \\n232:         internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\\\"window-timers\\\", windowSerializer, this);\\n233: \\n234:         triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\\n235:         processContext = new WindowContext(null);\\n236: \\n237:         windowAssignerContext =\\n238:                 new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\\n239:                     @Override\\n240:                     public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\\n241:                         return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\\n242:                     }\\n243:                 };\\n244: \\n245:         // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\\n246:         // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\\n247:         if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\\n248:             windowState =\\n249:                     (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\\n250:                             getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\\n251:         }\\n252: \\n253:         // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\\n254:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\\n255: \\n256:             // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\\n257:             if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\\n258:                 windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\\n259:             } else if (windowState != null) {\\n260:                 throw new IllegalStateException(\\n261:                         \\\"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\\\");\\n262:             }\\n263: \\n264:             @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n265:             final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\\n266: \\n267:             final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\\n268:                     new TupleSerializer<>(\\n269:                             typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\\n270: \\n271:             final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\\n272:                     new ListStateDescriptor<>(\\\"merging-window-set\\\", tupleSerializer);\\n273: \\n274:             // get the state that stores the merging sets\\n275:             mergingSetsState =\\n276:                     (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\\n277:                             getOrCreateKeyedState(\\n278:                                     VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\\n279:             mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\\n280:         }\\n281:     }\\n282: \\n283:     @Override\\n284:     public void close() throws Exception {\\n285:         super.close();\\n286:         timestampedCollector = null;\\n287:         triggerContext = null;\\n288:         processContext = null;\\n289:         windowAssignerContext = null;\\n290:     }\\n291: \\n292:     @Override\\n293:     public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\\n294:         final Collection<W> elementWindows =\\n295:                 windowAssigner.assignWindows(\\n296:                         element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\\n297: \\n298:         // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\\n299:         boolean isSkippedElement = true;\\n300: \\n301:         final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\\n302: \\n303:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\\n304:             MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\\n305: \\n306:             for (W window : elementWindows) {\\n307: \\n308:                 // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\\n309:                 // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\\n310:                 // actualWindow == window\\n311:                 W actualWindow =\\n312:                         mergingWindows.addWindow(\\n313:                                 window,\\n314:                                 new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\\n315:                                     @Override\\n316:                                     public void merge(\\n317:                                             W mergeResult,\\n318:                                             Collection<W> mergedWindows,\\n319:                                             W stateWindowResult,\\n320:                                             Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\\n321:                                             throws Exception {\\n322: \\n323:                                         if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\\n324:                                                 && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\\n325:                                                         <= internalTimerService\\n326:                                                                 .currentWatermark())) {\\n327:                                             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\\n328:                                                     \\\"The end timestamp of an \\\"\\n329:                                                             + \\\"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \\\"\\n330:                                                             + \\\"by merging. Current watermark: \\\"\\n331:                                                             + internalTimerService\\n332:                                                                     .currentWatermark()\\n333:                                                             + \\\" window: \\\"\\n334:                                                             + mergeResult);\\n335:                                         } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\\n336:                                             long currentProcessingTime =\\n337:                                                     internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\\n338:                                             if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\\n339:                                                     <= currentProcessingTime) {\\n340:                                                 throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\\n341:                                                         \\\"The end timestamp of a \\\"\\n342:                                                                 + \\\"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \\\"\\n343:                                                                 + \\\"by merging. Current processing time: \\\"\\n344:                                                                 + currentProcessingTime\\n345:                                                                 + \\\" window: \\\"\\n346:                                                                 + mergeResult);\\n347:                                             }\\n348:                                         }\\n349: \\n350:                                         triggerContext.key = key;\\n351:                                         triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\\n352: \\n353:                                         triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\\n354: \\n355:                                         for (W m : mergedWindows) {\\n356:                                             triggerContext.window = m;\\n357:                                             triggerContext.clear();\\n358:                                             deleteCleanupTimer(m);\\n359:                                         }\\n360: \\n361:                                         // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\\n362:                                         // state window\\n363:                                         windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\\n364:                                                 stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\\n365:                                     }\\n366:                                 });\\n367: \\n368:                 // drop if the window is already late\\n369:                 if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\\n370:                     mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\\n371:                     continue;\\n372:                 }\\n373:                 isSkippedElement = false;\\n374: \\n375:                 W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\\n376:                 if (stateWindow == null) {\\n377:                     throw new IllegalStateException(\\n378:                             \\\"Window \\\" + window + \\\" is not in in-flight window set.\\\");\\n379:                 }\\n380: \\n381:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\\n382:                 windowState.add(element.getValue());\\n383: \\n384:                 triggerContext.key = key;\\n385:                 triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\\n386: \\n387:                 TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\\n388: \\n389:                 if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\\n390:                     ACC contents = windowState.get();\\n391:                     if (contents != null) {\\n392:                         emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\\n393:                     }\\n394:                 }\\n395: \\n396:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\\n397:                     windowState.clear();\\n398:                 }\\n399:                 registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\\n400:             }\\n401: \\n402:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\\n403:             mergingWindows.persist();\\n404:         } else {\\n405:             for (W window : elementWindows) {\\n406: \\n407:                 // drop if the window is already late\\n408:                 if (isWindowLate(window)) {\\n409:                     continue;\\n410:                 }\\n411:                 isSkippedElement = false;\\n412: \\n413:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\\n414:                 windowState.add(element.getValue());\\n415: \\n416:                 triggerContext.key = key;\\n417:                 triggerContext.window = window;\\n418: \\n419:                 TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\\n420: \\n421:                 if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\\n422:                     ACC contents = windowState.get();\\n423:                     if (contents != null) {\\n424:                         emitWindowContents(window, contents);\\n425:                     }\\n426:                 }\\n427: \\n428:                 if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\\n429:                     windowState.clear();\\n430:                 }\\n431:                 registerCleanupTimer(window);\\n432:             }\\n433:         }\\n434: \\n435:         // side output input event if\\n436:         // element not handled by any window\\n437:         // late arriving tag has been set\\n438:         // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\\n439:         // element timestamp\\n440:         if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\\n441:             if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\\n442:                 sideOutput(element);\\n443:             } else {\\n444:                 this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\\n445:             }\\n446:         }\\n447:     }\\n448: \\n449:     @Override\\n450:     public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\\n451:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\\n452:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\\n453: \\n454:         MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\\n455: \\n456:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\\n457:             mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\\n458:             W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\\n459:             if (stateWindow == null) {\\n460:                 // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\\n461:                 // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\\n462:                 // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\\n463:                 return;\\n464:             } else {\\n465:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\\n466:             }\\n467:         } else {\\n468:             windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\\n469:             mergingWindows = null;\\n470:         }\\n471: \\n472:         TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\\n473: \\n474:         if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\\n475:             ACC contents = windowState.get();\\n476:             if (contents != null) {\\n477:                 emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\\n478:             }\\n479:         }\\n480: \\n481:         if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\\n482:             windowState.clear();\\n483:         }\\n484: \\n485:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\\n486:                 && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\\n487:             clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\\n488:         }\\n489: \\n490:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\\n491:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\\n492:             mergingWindows.persist();\\n493:         }\\n494:     }\\n495: \\n496:     @Override\\n497:     public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\\n498:         triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\\n499:         triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\\n500: \\n501:         MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\\n502: \\n503:         if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\\n504:             mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\\n505:             W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\\n506:             if (stateWindow == null) {\\n507:                 // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\\n508:                 // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\\n509:                 // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\\n510:                 return;\\n511:             } else {\\n512:                 windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\\n513:             }\\n514:         } else {\\n515:             windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\\n516:             mergingWindows = null;\\n517:         }\\n518: \\n519:         TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\\n520: \\n521:         if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\\n522:             ACC contents = windowState.get();\\n523:             if (contents != null) {\\n524:                 emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\\n525:             }\\n526:         }\\n527: \\n528:         if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\\n529:             windowState.clear();\\n530:         }\\n531: \\n532:         if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\\n533:                 && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\\n534:             clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\\n535:         }\\n536: \\n537:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\\n538:             // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\\n539:             mergingWindows.persist();\\n540:         }\\n541:     }\\n542: \\n543:     @Override\\n544:     public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\\n545:         boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\\n546:                 windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\\n547:                         && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\\n548:         return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\\n549:                 .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\\n550:                 .build();\\n551:     }\\n552: \\n553:     /**\\n554:      * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\\n555:      * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\\n556:      *\\n557:      * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\\n558:      * triggerContext object.\\n559:      */\\n560:     private void clearAllState(\\n561:             W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\\n562:             throws Exception {\\n563:         windowState.clear();\\n564:         triggerContext.clear();\\n565:         processContext.window = window;\\n566:         processContext.clear();\\n567:         if (mergingWindows != null) {\\n568:             mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\\n569:             mergingWindows.persist();\\n570:         }\\n571:     }\\n572: \\n573:     /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\\n574:     @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n575:     private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\\n576:         timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\\n577:         processContext.window = window;\\n578:         userFunction.process(\\n579:                 triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\\n580:     }\\n581: \\n582:     /**\\n583:      * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\\n584:      *\\n585:      * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\\n586:      */\\n587:     protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\\n588:         output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\\n589:     }\\n590: \\n591:     /**\\n592:      * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\\n593:      * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\\n594:      *\\n595:      * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\\n596:      * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\\n597:      */\\n598:     protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\\n599:         @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n600:         MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\\n601:                 (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\\n602:         return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\\n603:     }\\n604: \\n605:     /**\\n606:      * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\\n607:      * the given window.\\n608:      */\\n609:     protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\\n610:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\\n611:                 && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\\n612:     }\\n613: \\n614:     /**\\n615:      * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\\n616:      *\\n617:      * @param element The element to check\\n618:      * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\\n619:      */\\n620:     protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\\n621:         return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\\n622:                 && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\\n623:                         <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\\n624:     }\\n625: \\n626:     /**\\n627:      * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\\n628:      *\\n629:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\\n630:      */\\n631:     protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\\n632:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\\n633:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\\n634:             // don't set a GC timer for \\\"end of time\\\"\\n635:             return;\\n636:         }\\n637: \\n638:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\\n639:             triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n640:         } else {\\n641:             triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n642:         }\\n643:     }\\n644: \\n645:     /**\\n646:      * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\\n647:      *\\n648:      * @param window the window whose state to discard\\n649:      */\\n650:     protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\\n651:         long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\\n652:         if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\\n653:             // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\\n654:             return;\\n655:         }\\n656:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\\n657:             triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n658:         } else {\\n659:             triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\\n660:         }\\n661:     }\\n662: \\n663:     /**\\n664:      * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\\n665:      * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\\n666:      * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\\n667:      *\\n668:      * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\\n669:      */\\n670:     private long cleanupTime(W window) {\\n671:         if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\\n672:             long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\\n673:             return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\\n674:         } else {\\n675:             return window.maxTimestamp();\\n676:         }\\n677:     }\\n678: \\n679:     /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\\n680:     protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\\n681:         return time == cleanupTime(window);\\n682:     }\\n683: \\n684:     /**\\n685:      * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\\n686:      * state access for {@link\\n687:      * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\\n688:      */\\n689:     public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\\n690: \\n691:         // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\\n692:         // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\\n693:         protected W window;\\n694: \\n695:         public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\\n696:                 KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\\n697:             super(\\n698:                     keyedStateBackend,\\n699:                     new SerializerFactory() {\\n700:                         @Override\\n701:                         public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\\n702:                                 TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\\n703:                             return typeInformation.createSerializer(\\n704:                                     executionConfig.getSerializerConfig());\\n705:                         }\\n706:                     });\\n707:         }\\n708:     }\\n709: \\n710:     /**\\n711:      * Special {@link AbstractPerWindowStateStore} that doesn't allow access to per-window state.\\n712:      */\\n713:     public class MergingWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\\n714: \\n715:         public MergingWindowStateStore(\\n716:                 KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\\n717:             super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\\n718:         }\\n719: \\n720:         @Override\\n721:         public <T> ValueState<T> getState(ValueStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\\n722:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\\n723:                     \\\"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\\\");\\n724:         }\\n725: \\n726:         @Override\\n727:         public <T> ListState<T> getListState(ListStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\\n728:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\\n729:                     \\\"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\\\");\\n730:         }\\n731: \\n732:         @Override\\n733:         public <T> ReducingState<T> getReducingState(ReducingStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\\n734:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\\n735:                     \\\"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\\\");\\n736:         }\\n737: \\n738:         @Override\\n739:         public <IN, ACC, OUT> AggregatingState<IN, OUT> getAggregatingState(\\n740:                 AggregatingStateDescriptor<IN, ACC, OUT> stateProperties) {\\n741:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\\n742:                     \\\"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\\\");\\n743:         }\\n744: \\n745:         @Override\\n746:         public <UK, UV> MapState<UK, UV> getMapState(MapStateDescriptor<UK, UV> stateProperties) {\\n747:             throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\\n748:                     \\\"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\\\");\\n749:         }\\n750:     }\\n751: \\n752:     /**\\n753:      * Regular per-window state store for use with {@link\\n754:      * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\\n755:      */\\n756:     public class PerWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\\n757: \\n758:         public PerWindowStateStore(\\n759:                 KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\\n760:             super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\\n761:         }\\n762: \\n763:         @Override\\n764:         protected <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor)\\n765:                 throws Exception {\\n766:             return keyedStateBackend.getPartitionedState(window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\\n767:         }\\n768:     }\\n769: \\n770:     /**\\n771:      * A utility class for handling {@code ProcessWindowFunction} invocations. This can be reused by\\n772:      * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\\n773:      * {@code WindowContext}.\\n774:      */\\n775:     public class WindowContext implements InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext {\\n776:         protected W window;\\n777: \\n778:         protected AbstractPerWindowStateStore windowState;\\n779: \\n780:         public WindowContext(W window) {\\n781:             this.window = window;\\n782:             this.windowState =\\n783:                     windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner\\n784:                             ? new MergingWindowStateStore(\\n785:                                     getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig())\\n786:                             : new PerWindowStateStore(getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig());\\n787:         }\\n788: \\n789:         @Override\\n790:         public String toString() {\\n791:             return \\\"WindowContext{Window = \\\" + window.toString() + \\\"}\\\";\\n792:         }\\n793: \\n794:         public void clear() throws Exception {\\n795:             userFunction.clear(window, this);\\n796:         }\\n797: \\n798:         @Override\\n799:         public long currentProcessingTime() {\\n800:             return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\\n801:         }\\n802: \\n803:         @Override\\n804:         public long currentWatermark() {\\n805:             return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\\n806:         }\\n807: \\n808:         @Override\\n809:         public KeyedStateStore windowState() {\\n810:             this.windowState.window = this.window;\\n811:             return this.windowState;\\n812:         }\\n813: \\n814:         @Override\\n815:         public KeyedStateStore globalState() {\\n816:             return WindowOperator.this.getKeyedStateStore();\\n817:         }\\n818: \\n819:         @Override\\n820:         public <X> void output(OutputTag<X> outputTag, X value) {\\n821:             if (outputTag == null) {\\n822:                 throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"OutputTag must not be null.\\\");\\n823:             }\\n824:             output.collect(outputTag, new StreamRecord<>(value, window.maxTimestamp()));\\n825:         }\\n826:     }\\n827: \\n828:     /**\\n829:      * {@code Context} is a utility for handling {@code Trigger} invocations. It can be reused by\\n830:      * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\\n831:      * {@code Context}\\n832:      */\\n833:     public class Context implements Trigger.OnMergeContext {\\n834:         protected K key;\\n835:         protected W window;\\n836: \\n837:         protected Collection<W> mergedWindows;\\n838: \\n839:         public Context(K key, W window) {\\n840:             this.key = key;\\n841:             this.window = window;\\n842:         }\\n843: \\n844:         @Override\\n845:         public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\\n846:             return WindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\\n847:         }\\n848: \\n849:         public long getCurrentWatermark() {\\n850:             return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\\n851:         }\\n852: \\n853:         @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n854:         public <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\\n855:             try {\\n856:                 return WindowOperator.this.getPartitionedState(\\n857:                         window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\\n858:             } catch (Exception e) {\\n859:                 throw new RuntimeException(\\\"Could not retrieve state\\\", e);\\n860:             }\\n861:         }\\n862: \\n863:         @Override\\n864:         public <S extends MergingState<?, ?>> void mergePartitionedState(\\n865:                 StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\\n866:             if (mergedWindows != null && mergedWindows.size() > 0) {\\n867:                 try {\\n868:                     S rawState =\\n869:                             getKeyedStateBackend()\\n870:                                     .getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\\n871: \\n872:                     if (rawState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\\n873:                         @SuppressWarnings(\\\"unchecked\\\")\\n874:                         InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?> mergingState =\\n875:                                 (InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?>) rawState;\\n876:                         mergingState.mergeNamespaces(window, mergedWindows);\\n877:                     } else {\\n878:                         throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\n879:                                 \\\"The given state descriptor does not refer to a mergeable state (MergingState)\\\");\\n880:                     }\\n881:                 } catch (Exception e) {\\n882:                     throw new RuntimeException(\\\"Error while merging state.\\\", e);\\n883:                 }\\n884:             }\\n885:         }\\n886: \\n887:         @Override\\n888:         public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\\n889:             return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\\n890:         }\\n891: \\n892:         @Override\\n893:         public void registerProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\\n894:             internalTimerService.registerProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\\n895:         }\\n896: \\n897:         @Override\\n898:         public void registerEventTimeTimer(long time) {\\n899:             internalTimerService.registerEventTimeTimer(window, time);\\n900:         }\\n901: \\n902:         @Override\\n903:         public void deleteProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\\n904:             internalTimerService.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\\n905:         }\\n906: \\n907:         @Override\\n908:         public void deleteEventTimeTimer(long time) {\\n909:             internalTimerService.deleteEventTimeTimer(window, time);\\n910:         }\\n911: \\n912:         public TriggerResult onElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\\n913:             return trigger.onElement(element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), window, this);\\n914:         }\\n915: \\n916:         public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time) throws Exception {\\n917:             return trigger.onProcessingTime(time, window, this);\\n918:         }\\n919: \\n920:         public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time) throws Exception {\\n921:             return trigger.onEventTime(time, window, this);\\n922:         }\\n923: \\n924:         public void onMerge(Collection<W> mergedWindows) throws Exception {\\n925:             this.mergedWindows = mergedWindows;\\n926:             trigger.onMerge(window, this);\\n927:         }\\n928: \\n929:         public void clear() throws Exception {\\n930:             trigger.clear(window, this);\\n931:         }\\n932: \\n933:         @Override\\n934:         public String toString() {\\n935:             return \\\"Context{\\\" + \\\"key=\\\" + key + \\\", window=\\\" + window + '}';\\n936:         }\\n937:     }\\n938: \\n939:     /** Internal class for keeping track of in-flight timers. */\\n940:     protected static class Timer<K, W extends Window> implements Comparable<Timer<K, W>> {\\n941:         protected long timestamp;\\n942:         protected K key;\\n943:         protected W window;\\n944: \\n945:         public Timer(long timestamp, K key, W window) {\\n946:             this.timestamp = timestamp;\\n947:             this.key = key;\\n948:             this.window = window;\\n949:         }\\n950: \\n951:         @Override\\n952:         public int compareTo(Timer<K, W> o) {\\n953:             return Long.compare(this.timestamp, o.timestamp);\\n954:         }\\n955: \\n956:         @Override\\n957:         public boolean equals(Object o) {\\n958:             if (this == o) {\\n959:                 return true;\\n960:             }\\n961:             if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) {\\n962:                 return false;\\n963:             }\\n964: \\n965:             Timer<?, ?> timer = (Timer<?, ?>) o;\\n966: \\n967:             return timestamp == timer.timestamp\\n968:                     && key.equals(timer.key)\\n969:                     && window.equals(timer.window);\\n970:         }\\n971: \\n972:         @Override\\n973:         public int hashCode() {\\n974:             int result = (int) (timestamp ^ (timestamp >>> 32));\\n975:             result = 31 * result + key.hashCode();\\n976:             result = 31 * result + window.hashCode();\\n977:             return result;\\n978:         }\\n979: \\n980:         @Override\\n981:         public String toString() {\\n982:             return \\\"Timer{\\\"\\n983:                     + \\\"timestamp=\\\"\\n984:                     + timestamp\\n985:                     + \\\", key=\\\"\\n986:                     + key\\n987:                     + \\\", window=\\\"\\n988:                     + window\\n989:                     + '}';\\n990:         }\\n991:     }\\n992: \\n993:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n994:     // Getters for testing\\n995:     // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n996: \\n997:     @VisibleForTesting\\n998:     public Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> getTrigger() {\\n999:         return trigger;\\n1000:     }\\n1001: \\n1002:     @VisibleForTesting\\n1003:     public KeySelector<IN, K> getKeySelector() {\\n1004:         return keySelector;\\n1005:     }\\n1006: \\n1007:     @VisibleForTesting\\n1008:     public WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> getWindowAssigner() {\\n1009:         return windowAssigner;\\n1010:     }\\n1011: \\n1012:     @VisibleForTesting\\n1013:     public StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> getStateDescriptor() {\\n1014:         return windowStateDescriptor;\\n1015:     }\\n1016: }\\n1017: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/flink--0cc95fcc@de39c3e1777c43fe3363b3fd7784bf46b57a99c2/-/blob/flink-runtime/src/main/java/org/apache/flink/streaming/runtime/operators/windowing/WindowOperator.java\"}"
54. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one\n * or more contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file\n * distributed with this work for additional information\n * regarding copyright ownership.  The ASF licenses this file\n * to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the\n * \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance\n * with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\n\npackage org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing;\n\nimport org.apache.flink.annotation.Internal;\nimport org.apache.flink.annotation.VisibleForTesting;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.ExecutionConfig;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.functions.SerializerFactory;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AggregatingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AggregatingStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.AppendingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.KeyedStateStore;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ListStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.MapState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.MapStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.MergingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ReducingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ReducingStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.State;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.StateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ValueState;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.state.ValueStateDescriptor;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeinfo.TypeInformation;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.common.typeutils.TypeSerializer;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.functions.KeySelector;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.tuple.Tuple2;\nimport org.apache.flink.api.java.typeutils.runtime.TupleSerializer;\nimport org.apache.flink.metrics.Counter;\nimport org.apache.flink.metrics.MetricGroup;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.DefaultKeyedStateStore;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.KeyedStateBackend;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.VoidNamespace;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.VoidNamespaceSerializer;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalAppendingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalListState;\nimport org.apache.flink.runtime.state.internal.InternalMergingState;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.graph.StreamConfig;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.AbstractUdfStreamOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimer;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.InternalTimerService;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OneInputStreamOperator;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OperatorAttributes;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.OperatorAttributesBuilder;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.Output;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.TimestampedCollector;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.operators.Triggerable;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.GlobalWindows;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.MergingWindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.assigners.WindowAssigner;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.Trigger;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.triggers.TriggerResult;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.api.windowing.windows.Window;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.operators.windowing.functions.InternalWindowFunction;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.streamrecord.StreamRecord;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.tasks.ProcessingTimeService;\nimport org.apache.flink.streaming.runtime.tasks.StreamTask;\nimport org.apache.flink.util.OutputTag;\n\nimport java.util.Collection;\n\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkArgument;\nimport static org.apache.flink.util.Preconditions.checkNotNull;\n\n/**\n * An operator that implements the logic for windowing based on a {@link WindowAssigner} and {@link\n * Trigger}.\n *\n * <p>When an element arrives it gets assigned a key using a {@link KeySelector} and it gets\n * assigned to zero or more windows using a {@link WindowAssigner}. Based on this, the element is\n * put into panes. A pane is the bucket of elements that have the same key and same {@code Window}.\n * An element can be in multiple panes if it was assigned to multiple windows by the {@code\n * WindowAssigner}.\n *\n * <p>Each pane gets its own instance of the provided {@code Trigger}. This trigger determines when\n * the contents of the pane should be processed to emit results. When a trigger fires, the given\n * {@link InternalWindowFunction} is invoked to produce the results that are emitted for the pane to\n * which the {@code Trigger} belongs.\n *\n * @param <K> The type of key returned by the {@code KeySelector}.\n * @param <IN> The type of the incoming elements.\n * @param <OUT> The type of elements emitted by the {@code InternalWindowFunction}.\n * @param <W> The type of {@code Window} that the {@code WindowAssigner} assigns.\n */\n@Internal\npublic class WindowOperator<K, IN, ACC, OUT, W extends Window>\n        extends AbstractUdfStreamOperator<OUT, InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W>>\n        implements OneInputStreamOperator<IN, OUT>, Triggerable<K, W> {\n\n    private static final long serialVersionUID = 1L;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // Configuration values and user functions\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected final WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner;\n\n    private final KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector;\n\n    private final Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger;\n\n    private final StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor;\n\n    /** For serializing the key in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer;\n\n    /** For serializing the window in checkpoints. */\n    protected final TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer;\n\n    /**\n     * The allowed lateness for elements. This is used for:\n     *\n     * <ul>\n     *   <li>Deciding if an element should be dropped from a window due to lateness.\n     *   <li>Clearing the state of a window if the system time passes the {@code window.maxTimestamp\n     *       + allowedLateness} landmark.\n     * </ul>\n     */\n    protected final long allowedLateness;\n\n    /**\n     * {@link OutputTag} to use for late arriving events. Elements for which {@code\n     * window.maxTimestamp + allowedLateness} is smaller than the current watermark will be emitted\n     * to this.\n     */\n    protected final OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag;\n\n    private static final String LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME = \"numLateRecordsDropped\";\n\n    protected transient Counter numLateRecordsDropped;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that is not checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /** The state in which the window contents is stored. Each window is a namespace */\n    private transient InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowState;\n\n    /**\n     * The {@link #windowState}, typed to merging state for merging windows. Null if the window\n     * state is not mergeable.\n     */\n    private transient InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC> windowMergingState;\n\n    /** The state that holds the merging window metadata (the sets that describe what is merged). */\n    private transient InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsState;\n\n    /**\n     * This is given to the {@code InternalWindowFunction} for emitting elements with a given\n     * timestamp.\n     */\n    protected transient TimestampedCollector<OUT> timestampedCollector;\n\n    protected transient Context triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n\n    protected transient WindowContext processContext;\n\n    protected transient WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext windowAssignerContext;\n\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // State that needs to be checkpointed\n    // ------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    protected transient InternalTimerService<W> internalTimerService;\n\n    /** Creates a new {@code WindowOperator} based on the given policies and user functions. */\n    public WindowOperator(\n            WindowAssigner<? super IN, W> windowAssigner,\n            TypeSerializer<W> windowSerializer,\n            KeySelector<IN, K> keySelector,\n            TypeSerializer<K> keySerializer,\n            StateDescriptor<? extends AppendingState<IN, ACC>, ?> windowStateDescriptor,\n            InternalWindowFunction<ACC, OUT, K, W> windowFunction,\n            Trigger<? super IN, ? super W> trigger,\n            long allowedLateness,\n            OutputTag<IN> lateDataOutputTag) {\n\n        super(windowFunction);\n\n        checkArgument(allowedLateness >= 0);\n\n        checkArgument(\n                windowStateDescriptor == null || windowStateDescriptor.isSerializerInitialized(),\n                \"window state serializer is not properly initialized\");\n\n        this.windowAssigner = checkNotNull(windowAssigner);\n        this.windowSerializer = checkNotNull(windowSerializer);\n        this.keySelector = checkNotNull(keySelector);\n        this.keySerializer = checkNotNull(keySerializer);\n        this.windowStateDescriptor = windowStateDescriptor;\n        this.trigger = checkNotNull(trigger);\n        this.allowedLateness = allowedLateness;\n        this.lateDataOutputTag = lateDataOutputTag;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setup(\n            StreamTask<?, ?> containingTask,\n            StreamConfig config,\n            Output<StreamRecord<OUT>> output) {\n        super.setup(containingTask, config, output);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setProcessingTimeService(ProcessingTimeService processingTimeService) {\n        super.setProcessingTimeService(processingTimeService);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void open() throws Exception {\n        super.open();\n\n        this.numLateRecordsDropped = metrics.counter(LATE_ELEMENTS_DROPPED_METRIC_NAME);\n        timestampedCollector = new TimestampedCollector<>(output);\n\n        internalTimerService = getInternalTimerService(\"window-timers\", windowSerializer, this);\n\n        triggerContext = new Context(null, null);\n        processContext = new WindowContext(null);\n\n        windowAssignerContext =\n                new WindowAssigner.WindowAssignerContext() {\n                    @Override\n                    public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n                        return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                    }\n                };\n\n        // create (or restore) the state that hold the actual window contents\n        // NOTE - the state may be null in the case of the overriding evicting window operator\n        if (windowStateDescriptor != null) {\n            windowState =\n                    (InternalAppendingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, windowStateDescriptor);\n        }\n\n        // create the typed and helper states for merging windows\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n\n            // store a typed reference for the state of merging windows - sanity check\n            if (windowState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                windowMergingState = (InternalMergingState<K, W, IN, ACC, ACC>) windowState;\n            } else if (windowState != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\n                        \"The window uses a merging assigner, but the window state is not mergeable.\");\n            }\n\n            @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n            final Class<Tuple2<W, W>> typedTuple = (Class<Tuple2<W, W>>) (Class<?>) Tuple2.class;\n\n            final TupleSerializer<Tuple2<W, W>> tupleSerializer =\n                    new TupleSerializer<>(\n                            typedTuple, new TypeSerializer[] {windowSerializer, windowSerializer});\n\n            final ListStateDescriptor<Tuple2<W, W>> mergingSetsStateDescriptor =\n                    new ListStateDescriptor<>(\"merging-window-set\", tupleSerializer);\n\n            // get the state that stores the merging sets\n            mergingSetsState =\n                    (InternalListState<K, VoidNamespace, Tuple2<W, W>>)\n                            getOrCreateKeyedState(\n                                    VoidNamespaceSerializer.INSTANCE, mergingSetsStateDescriptor);\n            mergingSetsState.setCurrentNamespace(VoidNamespace.INSTANCE);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void close() throws Exception {\n        super.close();\n        timestampedCollector = null;\n        triggerContext = null;\n        processContext = null;\n        windowAssignerContext = null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void processElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n        final Collection<W> elementWindows =\n                windowAssigner.assignWindows(\n                        element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), windowAssignerContext);\n\n        // if element is handled by none of assigned elementWindows\n        boolean isSkippedElement = true;\n\n        final K key = this.<K>getKeyedStateBackend().getCurrentKey();\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n\n            // Check if element is late before attempting to merge\n            if (isElementLate(element)) {\n                if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                    sideOutput(element);\n                } else {\n                    this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = true;\n            } else {\n                // Process element with merging windows\n                for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                    // adding the new window might result in a merge, in that case the actualWindow\n                    // is the merged window and we work with that. If we don't merge then\n                    // actualWindow == window\n                    W actualWindow =\n                            mergingWindows.addWindow(\n                                    window,\n                                    new MergingWindowSet.MergeFunction<W>() {\n                                        @Override\n                                        public void merge(\n                                                W mergeResult,\n                                                Collection<W> mergedWindows,\n                                                W stateWindowResult,\n                                                Collection<W> mergedStateWindows)\n                                                throws Exception {\n\n                                            if ((windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                                                    && mergeResult.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                                                            <= internalTimerService\n                                                                    .currentWatermark())) {\n                                                throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                        \"The end timestamp of an \"\n                                                                + \"event-time window cannot become earlier than the current watermark \"\n                                                                + \"by merging. Current watermark: \"\n                                                                + internalTimerService\n                                                                        .currentWatermark()\n                                                                + \" window: \"\n                                                                + mergeResult);\n                                            } else if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n                                                long currentProcessingTime =\n                                                        internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n                                                if (mergeResult.maxTimestamp()\n                                                        <= currentProcessingTime) {\n                                                    throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                                                            \"The end timestamp of a \"\n                                                                    + \"processing-time window cannot become earlier than the current processing time \"\n                                                                    + \"by merging. Current processing time: \"\n                                                                    + currentProcessingTime\n                                                                    + \" window: \"\n                                                                    + mergeResult);\n                                                }\n                                            }\n\n                                            triggerContext.key = key;\n                                            triggerContext.window = mergeResult;\n\n                                            triggerContext.onMerge(mergedWindows);\n\n                                            for (W m : mergedWindows) {\n                                                triggerContext.window = m;\n                                                triggerContext.clear();\n                                                deleteCleanupTimer(m);\n                                            }\n\n                                            // merge the merged state windows into the newly resulting\n                                            // state window\n                                            windowMergingState.mergeNamespaces(\n                                                    stateWindowResult, mergedStateWindows);\n                                        }\n                                    });\n\n                    // drop if the window is already late\n                    if (isWindowLate(actualWindow)) {\n                        mergingWindows.retireWindow(actualWindow);\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                    W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(actualWindow);\n                    if (stateWindow == null) {\n                        throw new IllegalStateException(\n                                \"Window \" + window + \" is not in in-flight window set.\");\n                    }\n\n                    windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n                    windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                    triggerContext.key = key;\n                    triggerContext.window = actualWindow;\n\n                    TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                    if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                        ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                        if (contents != null) {\n                            emitWindowContents(actualWindow, contents);\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                        windowState.clear();\n                    }\n                    registerCleanupTimer(actualWindow);\n                }\n\n                // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n                mergingWindows.persist();\n            }\n        } else {\n            for (W window : elementWindows) {\n\n                // drop if the window is already late\n                if (isWindowLate(window)) {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                isSkippedElement = false;\n\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(window);\n                windowState.add(element.getValue());\n\n                triggerContext.key = key;\n                triggerContext.window = window;\n\n                TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onElement(element);\n\n                if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n                    ACC contents = windowState.get();\n                    if (contents != null) {\n                        emitWindowContents(window, contents);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n                    windowState.clear();\n                }\n                registerCleanupTimer(window);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // side output input event if\n        // element not handled by any window\n        // late arriving tag has been set\n        // windowAssigner is event time and current timestamp + allowed lateness no less than\n        // element timestamp\n        if (isSkippedElement && isElementLate(element)) {\n            if (lateDataOutputTag != null) {\n                sideOutput(element);\n            } else {\n                this.numLateRecordsDropped.inc();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onEventTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onEventTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void onProcessingTime(InternalTimer<K, W> timer) throws Exception {\n        triggerContext.key = timer.getKey();\n        triggerContext.window = timer.getNamespace();\n\n        MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows;\n\n        if (windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner) {\n            mergingWindows = getMergingWindowSet();\n            W stateWindow = mergingWindows.getStateWindow(triggerContext.window);\n            if (stateWindow == null) {\n                // Timer firing for non-existent window, this can only happen if a\n                // trigger did not clean up timers. We have already cleared the merging\n                // window and therefore the Trigger state, however, so nothing to do.\n                return;\n            } else {\n                windowState.setCurrentNamespace(stateWindow);\n            }\n        } else {\n            windowState.setCurrentNamespace(triggerContext.window);\n            mergingWindows = null;\n        }\n\n        TriggerResult triggerResult = triggerContext.onProcessingTime(timer.getTimestamp());\n\n        if (triggerResult.isFire()) {\n            ACC contents = windowState.get();\n            if (contents != null) {\n                emitWindowContents(triggerContext.window, contents);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if (triggerResult.isPurge()) {\n            windowState.clear();\n        }\n\n        if (!windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && isCleanupTime(triggerContext.window, timer.getTimestamp())) {\n            clearAllState(triggerContext.window, windowState, mergingWindows);\n        }\n\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            // need to make sure to update the merging state in state\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OperatorAttributes getOperatorAttributes() {\n        boolean isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream =\n                windowAssigner instanceof GlobalWindows\n                        && trigger instanceof GlobalWindows.EndOfStreamTrigger;\n        return new OperatorAttributesBuilder()\n                .setOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream(isOutputOnlyAfterEndOfStream)\n                .build();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Drops all state for the given window and calls {@link Trigger#clear(Window,\n     * Trigger.TriggerContext)}.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must ensure that the correct key is set in the state backend and the\n     * triggerContext object.\n     */\n    private void clearAllState(\n            W window, AppendingState<IN, ACC> windowState, MergingWindowSet<W> mergingWindows)\n            throws Exception {\n        windowState.clear();\n        triggerContext.clear();\n        processContext.window = window;\n        processContext.clear();\n        if (mergingWindows != null) {\n            mergingWindows.retireWindow(window);\n            mergingWindows.persist();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Emits the contents of the given window using the {@link InternalWindowFunction}. */\n    @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n    private void emitWindowContents(W window, ACC contents) throws Exception {\n        timestampedCollector.setAbsoluteTimestamp(window.maxTimestamp());\n        processContext.window = window;\n        userFunction.process(\n                triggerContext.key, window, processContext, contents, timestampedCollector);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write skipped late arriving element to SideOutput.\n     *\n     * @param element skipped late arriving element to side output\n     */\n    protected void sideOutput(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        output.collect(lateDataOutputTag, element);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Retrieves the {@link MergingWindowSet} for the currently active key. The caller must ensure\n     * that the correct key is set in the state backend.\n     *\n     * <p>The caller must also ensure to properly persist changes to state using {@link\n     * MergingWindowSet#persist()}.\n     */\n    protected MergingWindowSet<W> getMergingWindowSet() throws Exception {\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W> mergingAssigner =\n                (MergingWindowAssigner<? super IN, W>) windowAssigner;\n        return new MergingWindowSet<>(mergingAssigner, mergingSetsState);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns {@code true} if the watermark is after the end timestamp plus the allowed lateness of\n     * the given window.\n     */\n    protected boolean isWindowLate(W window) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime()\n                && (cleanupTime(window) <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark()));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Decide if a record is currently late, based on current watermark and allowed lateness.\n     *\n     * @param element The element to check\n     * @return The element for which should be considered when sideoutputs\n     */\n    protected boolean isElementLate(StreamRecord<IN> element) {\n        return (windowAssigner.isEventTime())\n                && (element.getTimestamp() + allowedLateness\n                        <= internalTimerService.currentWatermark());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Registers a timer to cleanup the content of the window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void registerCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // don't set a GC timer for \"end of time\"\n            return;\n        }\n\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.registerEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.registerProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Deletes the cleanup timer set for the contents of the provided window.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose state to discard\n     */\n    protected void deleteCleanupTimer(W window) {\n        long cleanupTime = cleanupTime(window);\n        if (cleanupTime == Long.MAX_VALUE) {\n            // no need to clean up because we didn't set one\n            return;\n        }\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            triggerContext.deleteEventTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        } else {\n            triggerContext.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(cleanupTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Returns the cleanup time for a window, which is {@code window.maxTimestamp +\n     * allowedLateness}. In case this leads to a value greater than {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} then a\n     * cleanup time of {@link Long#MAX_VALUE} is returned.\n     *\n     * @param window the window whose cleanup time we are computing.\n     */\n    private long cleanupTime(W window) {\n        if (windowAssigner.isEventTime()) {\n            long cleanupTime = window.maxTimestamp() + allowedLateness;\n            return cleanupTime >= window.maxTimestamp() ? cleanupTime : Long.MAX_VALUE;\n        } else {\n            return window.maxTimestamp();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /** Returns {@code true} if the given time is the cleanup time for the given window. */\n    protected final boolean isCleanupTime(W window, long time) {\n        return time == cleanupTime(window);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Base class for per-window {@link KeyedStateStore KeyedStateStores}. Used to allow per-window\n     * state access for {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public abstract class AbstractPerWindowStateStore extends DefaultKeyedStateStore {\n\n        // we have this in the base class even though it's not used in MergingKeyStore so that\n        // we can always set it and ignore what actual implementation we have\n        protected W window;\n\n        public AbstractPerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(\n                    keyedStateBackend,\n                    new SerializerFactory() {\n                        @Override\n                        public <T> TypeSerializer<T> createSerializer(\n                                TypeInformation<T> typeInformation) {\n                            return typeInformation.createSerializer(\n                                    executionConfig.getSerializerConfig());\n                        }\n                    });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Special {@link AbstractPerWindowStateStore} that doesn't allow access to per-window state.\n     */\n    public class MergingWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public MergingWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ValueState<T> getState(ValueStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ListState<T> getListState(ListStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <T> ReducingState<T> getReducingState(ReducingStateDescriptor<T> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <IN, ACC, OUT> AggregatingState<IN, OUT> getAggregatingState(\n                AggregatingStateDescriptor<IN, ACC, OUT> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <UK, UV> MapState<UK, UV> getMapState(MapStateDescriptor<UK, UV> stateProperties) {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException(\n                    \"Per-window state is not allowed when using merging windows.\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Regular per-window state store for use with {@link\n     * org.apache.flink.streaming.api.functions.windowing.ProcessWindowFunction}.\n     */\n    public class PerWindowStateStore extends AbstractPerWindowStateStore {\n\n        public PerWindowStateStore(\n                KeyedStateBackend<?> keyedStateBackend, ExecutionConfig executionConfig) {\n            super(keyedStateBackend, executionConfig);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor)\n                throws Exception {\n            return keyedStateBackend.getPartitionedState(window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * A utility class for handling {@code ProcessWindowFunction} invocations. This can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code WindowContext}.\n     */\n    public class WindowContext implements InternalWindowFunction.InternalWindowContext {\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected AbstractPerWindowStateStore windowState;\n\n        public WindowContext(W window) {\n            this.window = window;\n            this.windowState =\n                    windowAssigner instanceof MergingWindowAssigner\n                            ? new MergingWindowStateStore(\n                                    getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig())\n                            : new PerWindowStateStore(getKeyedStateBackend(), getExecutionConfig());\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public String toString() {\n            return \"WindowContext{Window = \" + window.toString() + \"}\";\n        }\n\n        public void clear() throws Exception {\n            userFunction.clear(window, this);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long currentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore windowState() {\n            this.windowState.window = this.window;\n            return this.windowState;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public KeyedStateStore globalState() {\n            return WindowOperator.this.getKeyedStateStore();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <X> void output(OutputTag<X> outputTag, X value) {\n            if (outputTag == null) {\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"OutputTag must not be null.\");\n            }\n            output.collect(outputTag, new StreamRecord<>(value, window.maxTimestamp()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * {@code Context} is a utility for handling {@code Trigger} invocations. It can be reused by\n     * setting the {@code key} and {@code window} fields. No internal state must be kept in the\n     * {@code Context}\n     */\n    public class Context implements Trigger.OnMergeContext {\n        protected K key;\n        protected W window;\n\n        protected Collection<W> mergedWindows;\n\n        public Context(K key, W window) {\n            this.key = key;\n            this.window = window;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public MetricGroup getMetricGroup() {\n            return WindowOperator.this.getMetricGroup();\n        }\n\n        public long getCurrentWatermark() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentWatermark();\n        }\n\n        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n        public <S extends State> S getPartitionedState(StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            try {\n                return WindowOperator.this.getPartitionedState(\n                        window, windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n            } catch (Exception e) {\n                throw new RuntimeException(\"Could not retrieve state\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public <S extends MergingState<?, ?>> void mergePartitionedState(\n                StateDescriptor<S, ?> stateDescriptor) {\n            if (mergedWindows != null && mergedWindows.size() > 0) {\n                try {\n                    S rawState =\n                            getKeyedStateBackend()\n                                    .getOrCreateKeyedState(windowSerializer, stateDescriptor);\n\n                    if (rawState instanceof InternalMergingState) {\n                        @SuppressWarnings(\"unchecked\")\n                        InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?> mergingState =\n                                (InternalMergingState<K, W, ?, ?, ?>) rawState;\n                        mergingState.mergeNamespaces(window, mergedWindows);\n                    } else {\n                        throw new IllegalArgumentException(\n                                \"The given state descriptor does not refer to a mergeable state (MergingState)\");\n                    }\n                } catch (Exception e) {\n                    throw new RuntimeException(\"Error while merging state.\", e);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public long getCurrentProcessingTime() {\n            return internalTimerService.currentProcessingTime();\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void registerEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.registerEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteProcessingTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteProcessingTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void deleteEventTimeTimer(long time) {\n            internalTimerService.deleteEventTimeTimer(window, time);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onElement(StreamRecord<IN> element) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onElement(element.getValue(), element.getTimestamp(), window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onProcessingTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onProcessingTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public TriggerResult onEventTime(long time) throws Exception {\n            return trigger.onEventTime(time, window, this);\n        }\n\n        public void onMerge(Collection<W> mergedWindows) throws Exception {\n            this.mergedWindows = mergedWindows;\n            trigger.onMerge(window, this);\n        }\n\n
